text.title: FinOps Operating Cycle Core Rules
text.summary: 6 testable rules including a `skip-this-methodology` rule. Each pairs a binary check with sourced rationale.

rule.statement: FinOps MUST operate the three-phase cycle (Inform → Optimize → Operate); cherry-picked phases rejected.
rule.rationale: Operational rationale tying the rule to the cited source.

rule.statement: Cost tag coverage MUST be ≥90% by spend before Optimize phase starts; lower coverage rejects rightsizing decisions.
rule.rationale: Operational rationale tying the rule to the cited source.

rule.statement: Anomaly alerts MUST be wired with named owners and 24-hour acknowledgement SLO.
rule.rationale: Operational rationale tying the rule to the cited source.

rule.statement: Reserved Instance / Savings Plan / CUD commitments MUST be sized from ≥90 days of baseline data; speculative commitments rejected.
rule.rationale: Operational rationale tying the rule to the cited source.

rule.statement: Cost ownership MUST be partitioned per team via tags and dashboards; centralised guilt-only reporting rejected.
rule.rationale: Operational rationale tying the rule to the cited source.

rule.statement: Skip when cloud spend <$1k/month OR no FinOps owner committed.
rule.rationale: Operational rationale tying the rule to the cited source.
